column1: Researchers have found that REF6045 as an active operator assisted banking fraud campaign targeting customers of banks
The attack begins with ClickFix style fake CAPTCHA pages that trick victims into running a malicious command
column2: leading to the download and execution of the SCMBANKER toolkit.
the malware displays a fake Windows Update screen
column4: and limits mouse movement to distract the victim while additional components are installed.

column1: SCMBANKER downloads its supporting components and establishes persistence through Registry Run keys and Startup Folder entries
column2: allowing it to remain active after system restarts.

column1: The toolkit communicates with a command and control server to collect system information
column4: giving operators visibility into the victim's actions.

column1: When victims access targeted banking services
column2: the malware presents fake bank security alerts and support messages designed to create a sense of urgency
column3: prompting them to engage with attacker controlled operators and facilitating vishing based fraud.

column1: The malware continuously monitors browser activity and can redirect selected victims to attacker controlled phishing websites designed to capture banking related information.
The toolkit monitors the clipboard for copied bank account and payment card details and can replace them with attacker controlled values
column2: redirecting financial transactions to accounts controlled by the threat actor.
the attackers deploy a legitimate remote access tool and use additional capabilities for keystroke monitoring and remote command execution
